title: Classroom Communication & Data Safety
description: Test your understanding of safe communication practices, approved channels, and protecting sensitive student information.

questions.prompt: Why is classroom communication a cybersecurity concern?

choices.text: Because teachers should never communicate with anyone
Because communication often involves sensitive student information that can be exposed if handled incorrectly
Because email is no longer used in schools
Because only IT staff are allowed to discuss student issues

questions.explanation: Classroom communication often includes sensitive student information, so mistakes can create privacy and data exposure risks.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is the best communication principle when discussing student matters?

choices.text: Share as much detail as possible to avoid confusion
Share only the information necessary for the situation
Always include every student detail in writing
Use personal messaging apps to speed things up

questions.explanation: Sharing only what is necessary reduces the risk of exposing private student information.

questions.prompt: Scenario: A teacher is emailing a parent about a student's missing work and behavioral concerns. While typing the address, autocomplete selects the wrong parent with a similar last name, and the teacher sends the message before noticing. Question: What is the main problem in this situation?

choices.text: The email was too short
Sensitive student information was sent to the wrong recipient
The parent may not like email
The message should have included more attachments

questions.explanation: Sending student information to the wrong recipient is a privacy and data exposure incident.

questions.prompt: Scenario: A teacher is emailing a parent about a student's missing work and behavioral concerns. While typing the address, autocomplete selects the wrong parent with a similar last name, and the teacher sends the message before noticing. Question: What should the teacher do next?

choices.text: Ignore it and hope no one notices
Send the same message to more parents to make it less noticeable
Report the mistake according to school policy and notify the appropriate administrator or privacy contact
Delete the sent email from the inbox and assume the issue is solved

questions.explanation: Communication mistakes involving student information should be reported promptly so the school can respond appropriately.

questions.prompt: True or False: Using a personal email account is always acceptable if it is faster than the school system.

choices.text: True
False

questions.explanation: Student-related communication should go through approved school channels unless school policy clearly allows otherwise.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is a safer communication practice?

choices.text: Using approved school communication tools and checking recipients before sending
Posting student details in a large informal chat for quick feedback
Using social media direct messages for parent communication
Sharing grade documents with open permissions for convenience

questions.explanation: Approved tools and recipient verification help reduce privacy and data exposure risks.

questions.prompt: Scenario: A teacher creates a spreadsheet containing student grades and comments, then shares it using a link set to Anyone with the link can view. The document link is later copied into a larger staff thread. Question: What is the biggest risk in this situation?

choices.text: The spreadsheet may look unprofessional
The document permissions may allow unauthorized people to access student information
The file may take too long to load
The comments may be too detailed for grading

questions.explanation: Incorrect sharing permissions can expose student data to people who should not have access.

questions.prompt: Scenario: A teacher creates a spreadsheet containing student grades and comments, then shares it using a link set to Anyone with the link can view. The document link is later copied into a larger staff thread. Question: What is the best prevention for this situation?

choices.text: Avoid using documents altogether
Set the document so only the intended authorized people can view or edit it before sharing
Use more colors in the spreadsheet
Send the same link to parents so everyone has equal access

questions.explanation: Document permissions should be limited to the specific authorized audience before sharing.

questions.prompt: True or False: Casual staff chats or conversations in public areas can still create student privacy risks.

choices.text: True
False

questions.explanation: Sensitive student information can be exposed through casual conversations, informal chats, or public settings.

questions.prompt: What is the best response if you think you shared student information through the wrong communication channel?

choices.text: Ignore it if your intentions were good
Report the issue according to school policy and notify the appropriate staff
Post a public explanation of what happened
Continue using the same communication method but be more careful next time

questions.explanation: Prompt reporting helps the school assess the situation and reduce further exposure.
